q.en: What fact does the book give about the brain's weight and energy use?
q.zh: 关于大脑的重量和能量消耗，书中给出了什么事实？

options.en: It uses less than one percent of the body's energy
options.zh: 它消耗不到身体百分之一的能量

options.en: It is the single heaviest organ in the whole body
options.zh: 它是全身最重的一个器官

options.en: It is 2% of body weight but uses over 20% of energy
options.zh: 它只占体重的2%，却消耗超过20%的能量

options.en: It weighs more than all the other body parts combined according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 它比身体其余所有部分加起来还重（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: It is 2% of body weight but uses over 20% of energy
explain.zh: 它只占体重的2%，却消耗超过20%的能量

q.en: About how many neurons does the human brain have?
q.zh: 人的大脑大约有多少个神经元？

options.en: A number so huge that no person could ever count them
options.zh: 一个大到没人能数清的数目

options.en: About 86 billion neurons
options.zh: 约860亿个神经元

options.en: Exactly one thousand neurons in total
options.zh: 总共正好一千个神经元

options.en: Only about one million tiny neurons
options.zh: 只有大约一百万个微小神经元

q.en: Why is the brain stem sometimes called the lizard brain?
q.zh: 为什么脑干部有时被称为“蜥蜴脑”？

options.en: It is the thinking part of the brain that solves hard math according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 它是负责解难题的思考部位（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: It is the wrinkled outside layer of the brain
options.zh: 它是大脑起皱的外层

options.en: It stores only your oldest long-term memories
options.zh: 它只储存你最久远的长期记忆

options.en: It controls basic survival like breathing and heartbeat
options.zh: 它控制呼吸、心跳等基本的生存机能

explain.en: It controls basic survival like breathing and heartbeat
explain.zh: 它控制呼吸、心跳等基本的生存机能

q.en: What does the cerebellum mainly do?

options.en: It controls your speech, your reading, and your writing
options.zh: 它控制你的说话、阅读和书写

options.en: It pumps blood to every part of the body
options.zh: 它把血液泵向身体各处

options.en: It coordinates smooth muscle movement and balance
options.zh: 它协调流畅的肌肉运动和平衡

options.en: It gently digests the food you eat each day
options.zh: 它慢慢消化你每天吃的食物

explain.en: It coordinates smooth muscle movement and balance
explain.zh: 它协调流畅的肌肉运动和平衡

options.en: A special kind of brain cell that carries signals
options.zh: 一种传递信号的特殊脑细胞

options.en: The bundle connecting the two brain hemispheres
options.zh: 连接左右两个大脑半球的神经束

options.en: The smallest and lowest part of the brain stem
options.zh: 脑干最小最下的部分

options.en: The hard bone at the top of the spine that protects the brain
options.zh: 脊柱顶端保护大脑的硬骨

explain.en: The bundle connecting the two brain hemispheres
explain.zh: 连接左右两个大脑半球的神经束

q.zh: 菲尼亚斯·盖奇身上发生了什么？

options.en: He invented the very first EEG machine back in 1920
options.zh: 他在1920年发明了第一台脑电图机

options.en: He was a famous French scientist who first mapped the whole brain
options.zh: 他是首位绘制全脑地图的法国科学家

options.en: He once flew a hot-air balloon across the open ocean
options.zh: 他曾乘热气球飞越开阔的海洋

options.en: A rod shot through his frontal lobe, changing his personality
options.zh: 一根铁棍射穿他的额叶，改变了他的性格

explain.en: A rod shot through his frontal lobe, changing his personality
explain.zh: 一根铁棍射穿他的额叶，改变了他的性格

q.en: How are an EEG and an fMRI different?
q.zh: 脑电图（EEG）和功能磁共振（fMRI）有何不同？

options.en: EEG measures electrical signals; fMRI measures blood flow
options.zh: EEG测量电信号，fMRI测量血流

options.en: The EEG uses strong magnets; the fMRI uses electricity
options.zh: EEG用强磁，fMRI用电

options.en: The fMRI was invented many years before the EEG
options.zh: fMRI比EEG早很多年发明

options.en: Both machines measure exactly the same thing in the same way
options.zh: 两台机器测量的是完全相同的事物、方式也一样

explain.en: EEG measures electrical signals; fMRI measures blood flow

q.en: According to the book, what helps keep your brain healthy?
q.zh: 按书中所说，什么有助于保持大脑健康？

options.en: Only taking one special kind of vitamin pill every day
options.zh: 每天只吃一种特殊的维生素药丸

options.en: Exercise, sleep, brain foods, and puzzles
options.zh: 运动、睡眠、健脑食物和益智游戏

options.en: Avoiding all forms of physical activity
options.zh: 避免一切形式的体力活动

options.en: Sleeping only two short hours each single night
options.zh: 每晚只睡短短两小时

explain.en: Exercise, sleep, brain foods, and puzzles
explain.zh: 运动、睡眠、健脑食物和益智游戏

q.en: Why does the book compare the brain to a computer?
q.zh: 书中为什么把大脑和电脑作比较？

options.en: To show brains learn and adapt better than computers
options.zh: 为了说明大脑比电脑更善于学习和适应

options.en: To teach readers how to build a working robot brain
options.zh: 为了教读者如何造一个能运作的机器脑

options.en: To claim the human brain is made entirely of metal
options.zh: 为了声称人脑完全由金属构成

options.en: To prove that computers will always be much smarter than us
options.zh: 为了证明电脑永远比我们聪明得多

explain.en: To show brains learn and adapt better than computers
explain.zh: 为了说明大脑比电脑更善于学习和适应

q.en: What is a concussion, as described in the book?
q.zh: 书中说的脑震荡是什么？

options.en: A brain injury from a hard blow to the head
options.zh: 头部受到重击造成的脑损伤

options.en: A painful disease that only the very oldest people get
options.zh: 只有最年长者才会得的痛苦疾病

options.en: A strange kind of dream during very deep sleep
options.zh: 深睡时做的一种怪梦

options.en: A sick stomach caused by eating far too much bad food
options.zh: 吃太多坏东西引起的肠胃不适

explain.en: A brain injury from a hard blow to the head
explain.zh: 头部受到重击造成的脑损伤

prompt.en: Which brain-care habit from the book would be hardest for you to follow, and what small change could make it easier?
prompt.zh: 书中提到的哪种健脑习惯对你最难做到？你能做哪一个小改变让它更容易坚持？

en: Name the habit you find hardest
Say why it is difficult for you
Suggest one small change to start
zh: 说出你觉得最难的习惯
提出一个可以马上开始的小改变
